states_list.name: AUTH_PENDING
states_list.description: Username provided, waiting for password authentication

states_list.name: DATA_TRANSFER
states_list.description: Data connection open for file or directory listing transfer

states_list.name: RENAME_PENDING
states_list.description: Rename operation initiated, waiting for target filename

states_list.name: CLOSED
states_list.description: Session terminated by client or server

invariants_list.rule: PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
USER and PASS must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR) are issued.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be sent before a data transfer command (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, APPE).
USER may be resent after a failed PASS attempt.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
QUIT is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
USER.username: Argument must be a username string
PASS.password: Argument must be a password string
TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary
PORT.address_port: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components)
CWD.path: Argument must be a valid directory path
RETR.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
STOR.STOR: Argument must be a valid filename or path

invariants_created: PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
USER and PASS must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR) are issued.
A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be sent before a data transfer command (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, APPE).
USER may be resent after a failed PASS attempt.
RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
QUIT is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
USER.username: Argument must be a username string
PASS.password: Argument must be a password string
TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary
PORT.address_port: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components)
CWD.path: Argument must be a valid directory path
RETR.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
STOR.STOR: Argument must be a valid filename or path

status_changes.description: INIT -> AUTH_PENDING via USER
status_changes.rationale: USER command typically results in a 331 response requesting a password.

status_changes.description: AUTH_PENDING -> AUTHENTICATED via PASS
status_changes.rationale: Successful PASS command results in a 230 response indicating login success.

status_changes.description: AUTH_PENDING -> AUTH_PENDING via PASS
status_changes.rationale: A 530 response indicates failure but usually allows for re-attempt or re-entry of credentials in the same pending context.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via LIST
status_changes.rationale: LIST command returns 150 when initiating a data transfer for directory listings.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via RETR
status_changes.rationale: RETR command returns 150 when initiating a data transfer for file retrieval.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> DATA_TRANSFER via STOR
status_changes.rationale: STOR command returns 150 when initiating a data transfer for file upload.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> RENAME_PENDING via RNFR
status_changes.rationale: RNFR returns 350 to indicate the command was accepted and the server is waiting for RNTO.

status_changes.description: RENAME_PENDING -> AUTHENTICATED via RNTO
status_changes.rationale: RNTO returns 250 to indicate the rename operation is complete, returning the session to a standard authenticated state.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> CLOSED via QUIT
status_changes.rationale: QUIT returns 221 and closes the connection.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> INIT via REIN
status_changes.rationale: REIN (Reinitialize) is designed to reset the session state. 220 is the expected success response code.

status_changes.description: INIT -> CLOSED via QUIT
status_changes.rationale: QUIT is allowed at the start of a session.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> AUTHENTICATED via CWD
status_changes.rationale: CWD is a self-loop on the authenticated state returning 250.

status_changes.description: AUTHENTICATED -> AUTHENTICATED via PWD
status_changes.rationale: PWD is a self-loop on the authenticated state returning 257.

status_changes.description: PASS command must be preceded by a USER command.
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP sequencing documented in RFC 959 and supported by the provided trace evidence.

status_changes.description: USER and PASS must be successfully completed before data transfer commands (LIST, RETR, STOR) are issued.
status_changes.rationale: Authentication is a prerequisite for most file operations in FTP.

status_changes.description: A data connection establishment command (PASV, PORT, EPSV, or EPRT) must be sent before a data transfer command (LIST, NLST, RETR, STOR, APPE).
status_changes.rationale: FTP requires a setup of the data channel (active or passive) before transferring data.

status_changes.description: USER may be resent after a failed PASS attempt.
status_changes.rationale: Standard behavior to allow re-authentication attempts after failure (530).

status_changes.description: RNTO must be preceded by RNFR.
status_changes.rationale: The rename sequence is defined as RNFR (350) followed by RNTO.

status_changes.description: QUIT is valid at any point in the session to terminate the connection.
status_changes.rationale: Standard protocol behavior for session termination.

status_changes.description: USER.username: Argument must be a username string
status_changes.rationale: Basic syntactic requirement for the USER command.

status_changes.description: PASS.password: Argument must be a password string
status_changes.rationale: Basic syntactic requirement for the PASS command.

status_changes.description: TYPE.type_code: Must be 'A' for ASCII or 'I' for Image/Binary
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP type codes.

status_changes.description: PORT.address_port: Format: h1,h2,h3,h4,p1,p2 (IP and port components)
status_changes.rationale: Standard FTP PORT command format.

status_changes.description: CWD.path: Argument must be a valid directory path
status_changes.rationale: Functional requirement for directory navigation.

status_changes.description: RETR.filename: Argument must be a valid filename or path
status_changes.rationale: Requirement for file retrieval.

status_changes.description: STOR.STOR: Argument must be a valid filename or path
status_changes.rationale: The field name appears duplicated (STOR.STOR) but the semantic meaning for the STOR command argument is correct.
